prompt: The cast throws ClassCastException at runtime. What is the bug?
choices: Animal cannot be cast to Cat because Animal does not extend Cat
The runtime type of a is Dog; casting to Cat is invalid because Dog is not a Cat, causing ClassCastException
The cast syntax (Cat) a is illegal when a is declared as Animal
ClassCastException only occurs when casting to a primitive type
explanation: The compiler allows the cast because Cat and Dog are both Animals — it cannot prove it will fail at compile time. At runtime, the JVM checks whether the actual object (a Dog) is a Cat. It isn't, so ClassCastException is thrown. Fix: use instanceof before casting.
